title: Virtual Subcommittee Meetings for NAHB’s Spring Leadership Meeting Begin Next Week

title: Podcast: What War and Fed Changes Mean for Housing Market and Economy
summary: On the latest episode of NAHB’s podcast, Housing Developments, CEO Jim Tobin and COO Paul Lopez are joined by Chief Economist Dr. Robert Dietz to discuss the latest economic news and what it means for housing.

title: Rescinded Energy Code Mandate Major Win for NAHB and Housing Affordability
summary: HUD and the Department of Agriculture (USDA) announced this week that they are rescinding a requirement that imposed the 2021 International Energy Conservation Code (IECC) and ASHRAE 90.1-2019 as the minimum energy-efficiency standards for certain single-family and multifamily housing programs.

title: Make Your Voice Heard: Take the Industry Pulse Check Today
summary: As NAHB builds toward our 100th anniversary, we’ve launched Blueprint to 100, a bold effort to strengthen and modernize the Federation. At the center of this initiative is the Industry Pulse Check — the largest listening and learning initiative in NAHB’s history. And it only works if you’re part of it.

title: Pause Work on Your Jobsites Next Week to Review Fall Prevention Safety Procedures
summary: OSHA’s annual National Safety Stand-Down to prevent falls in construction begins Monday, May 4. Take a few minutes during the week to pause work and use valuable resources from OSHA and NAHB to educate workers on fall risks and safety procedures to help avoid preventable injuries.

title: Fed Changes Leadership, but Powell Stays On Board
summary: Fed Chair Jerome Powell is not expected to fully step away from the Federal Reserve. Instead, he plans to continue serving as a member of the Board of Governors as long as the Justice Department's probe into the cost of the Fed’s headquarters renovations remains active.

title: Indiana Students Explore Career Paths Beyond a Four-Year Degree
summary: The Builders Association of Elkhart County (Ind.) connects local students with hands‑on construction experiences that open their eyes to career paths beyond a four‑year degree.

title: Home Building Shows Signs of Stabilization with Monthly Gain in Starts
summary: Overall housing starts increased 10.8% in March to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 1.5 million units, according to a report from the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development and the U.S. Census Bureau.

title: NAHB Applauds HUD and USDA Action to Roll Back Costly Energy Mandate
summary: NAHB Chairman Bill Owens issued the following statement after the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) and the Department of Agriculture (USDA) announcement today to rescind the rule that would impose the 2021 International Energy Conservation Code (IECC) and ASHRAE 90.1-2019 as the minimum energy-efficiency standards for certain single-family and multifamily housing programs.

title: Shrinking Share of Tradesmen in the Construction Workforce
summary: The American construction labor force is continuing its momentum away from construction trades and towards management, business and technical roles, according to NAHB’s analysis of the latest 2024 data from the American Community Survey (ACS).

title: U.S. Customs Announces First Phase to Apply for Tariff Refunds
summary: The U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) has launched a new landing page for “International Emergency Economic Powers (IEEPA) Duty Refunds,” rolling out a process for seeking recoupment of IEEPA tariffs. Only importers of record and authorized customs brokers can apply for tariff refunds, and many questions regarding refunds remain unanswered.

title: Population Growth and Housing Supply Dynamics at the County Level
summary: U.S. population growth slowed notably in the latest population estimates from the U.S. Census Bureau, with the nation expanding by just 0.5% in 2025, roughly half the pace of the prior year.

title: Housing’s ‘Silver Tsunami’ Is Coming, But It Won’t Hit Every Market

title: Blueprint to 100: Industry Pulse Check Launches May 1

title: Labor Department Proposes New Joint Employer Rule for Wage and Hour Enforcement

title: NAHB Applauds Lawmakers’ Push to Remove Harmful Mandate from Major Housing Package

title: NAHB and ICC Release 2025 Edition of the National Green Building Standard®
